text:
I absolutely LOVE my new wall vinyl.
PROS:Great seller communication! Had a question about colors before product was shipped and seller very quickly responded to show/ explain to me what the difference in the two colors I had was!
Came shipped in a hard cardboard tube, product came rolled up with instructions clearly explaining installation. I didn’t follow them exactly, I don’t have the patience to put up one wait over 24hrs then repeat- I placed the vinyl on the wall lined up with with my markings after checking it a bajillion times to be sure it was exactly where I wanted it, rubbed over it vigorously for a few minutes and SLOWLY peeled back the top paper while keeping my hand pressed right below it guiding it over the vinyl. Go SLOW, my name is very long and loopy (think numerous a, n, e,l,i) the letters are whispy, elegant and beautiful. I did not have any issues with letters tearing or it not coming off the backing.
I am completely psycho when it comes to things being off level so I took my time making sure everything was going to line up exactly how I wanted- best part was that all the pieces came on their own paper so I could decide where to place them according to my space and design taste. Leading into my only con, the paper for the vinyl was cut crooked. Not a problem though, just a detail that if you’re in a hurry and not paying attention could lead to a crooked vinyl. A few quick snips with my scissors fixed the tiny inconvenience.

All in all, I’m going to order more from this company. I absolutely adore my sticker- and if I wasn’t so easily identifiable by my very unique name I would post so many photos because I am so proud of how this turned out!

text:
Dear Trip Machine Co,

Unfortunately I will have to give you a bad review on amazon. I purchased leather grip wraps in the vintage tan color. The quality of the product was perfect, but the glue on the leather made a mess and ruined the final look.

I saw all the tutorials you have in your webpage and others similar on the internet. All the tutorial shows the product that came with an extra piece of 3M tape to finish de installation, but this time, the wraps came with “super glue” to apply to the edges to seal them. Unfortunately, the glue spread out on the leather wrap, making it look very messy …. My advice is not to use glue in the future, because as soon as it touches the leather, all the fine vintage color, ends up ruined.

I have some pictures if you like, but in the end I will have to take off the wraps and lose my money. I would like a refund if possible.

Thanks

text: I read many, many reviews to try and glean some sort of best process to do this for those that had success with these.  I finally got time to get mine on the wall (and they look fantastic, I'm so happy with the end result) and this is what worked for me: flattening the large roll of paper that the stickers come on, cutting out the shapes and hanging up with painters tape before applying, take pictures once you have your preferred pattern (painters tape only holds for so long), have a buddy helping out to keep you sane.  Each person will have their own idea for layout, but if you want clusters like I did (in pic), really think about what needs to go down first because while you can lift and replace on the wall ok, maybe once or twice, the stickers stick REALLY WELL to each other and are not as forgiving.  I put down leaves first and then went flowers, placing down any leaves that I had overlapping as needed.  It worked best for me to start a sticker and keep the back on and kind of roll that paper up as I set the sticker on the wall, especially the bigger flowers.  I also bought tools to help smooth them out and prevent air bubbles as I pressed them down, it helped so much.  One reviewer suggested to heat set with a hairdryer if you plan to have them up for a long time, I did that, seemed to help.  They've been up for about 2 weeks now with no problems, no hint of peeling up or falling off the wall.  This went in my smaller room that I use as a home office/guest bedroom and I used every sticker from the 2 sets that I ordered.  Took me about 6-8 hours total with help (slow and steady).  Very happy with how it turned out.

description: ['This is an upcycled print printed on an old dictionary page. We take old books about to be destroyed and upcycle them in order to give them new life. The aging, slightly-yellowed page is carefully removed, and the image is printed directly on the recycled paper. The result is a unique and absolutely beautiful print suitable for framing. These prints are definite conversation-starters and look just gorgeous while hanging on the wall. The pages are approximately 8.5 x 11 in size, with a very slight variation. No two are exactly the same, but all will fit nicely in an 8.5 x 11 frame. You will not get the exact dictionary page shown in the image, but you will get a print from the same book. Every piece is original. ***Frame is not included****. You will receive just the print. These vintage book prints make awesome, unique gifts and are environmentally responsible. All prints are shipped flat in rigid, stay-flat mailers and are protected in plastic sleeves.']
